channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump says war in Iran could last for weeks or longer
item.description: President Trump spoke live for the first time Monday since the U.S. and Israel began striking Iran, sharing his administration's objectives for the country.
item.content:encoded: President Trump spoke live for the first time Monday since the U.S. and Israel began striking Iran, sharing his administration's objectives for the country.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Congress expected to vote on war power measures amid the war with Iran
item.description: Lawmakers are again pushing for votes on bills to reclaim congressional authority to declare wars. Previous efforts have failed, but lawmakers hope for renewed support amid the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: Lawmakers are again pushing for votes on bills to reclaim congressional authority to declare wars. Previous efforts have failed, but lawmakers hope for renewed support amid the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Sen. Chris Coons, D-Del., talks about the war with Iran and upcoming war powers vote
item.description: NPR's A Martínez asks Delaware Democrat Chris Coons, a member of the Senate Foreign Affairs Committee, about the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martínez asks Delaware Democrat Chris Coons, a member of the Senate Foreign Affairs Committee, about the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: How President Trump's views on foreign intervention has changed over the years
item.description: Long before he became a presidential candidate and during his first term, Donald Trump made avoiding foreign wars a cornerstone of his agenda. But his position on foreign intervention has shifted.
item.content:encoded: Long before he became a presidential candidate and during his first term, Donald Trump made avoiding foreign wars a cornerstone of his agenda. But his position on foreign intervention has shifted.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Trump promised the MAGA base no new wars. Then he went to war with Iran
item.description: President Trump promised his "Make America Great Again" voters an "America First" foreign policy. With the war in Iran, he's testing MAGA world's willingness to be flexible on one of its core beliefs.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump promised his "Make America Great Again" voters an "America First" foreign policy. With the war in Iran, he's testing MAGA world's willingness to be flexible on one of its core beliefs.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. urges Americans across Middle East to leave as war with Iran intensifies, Congress expected to vote on Trump's war powers, voters head to polls for Senate primaries in North Carolina and Texas.
item.content:encoded: U.S. urges Americans across Middle East to leave as war with Iran intensifies, Congress expected to vote on Trump's war powers, voters head to polls for Senate primaries in North Carolina and Texas.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Trump wants to reach 1 million apprenticeships. Arkansas is jumpstarting the effort
item.description: The Trump administration has a goal of reaching 1 million registered apprenticeships. It's put Arkansas in charge of leading the way.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration has a goal of reaching 1 million registered apprenticeships. It's put Arkansas in charge of leading the way.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Freedom Plane flies historical docs across the U.S. for 250th birthday celebration
item.description: The National Archives Freedom Plane will make Kansas City its first stop on its national tour. The plane will carry some of America's most significant founding-era documents. The tour is in honor of America's 250th anniversary.
item.content:encoded: The National Archives Freedom Plane will make Kansas City its first stop on its national tour. The plane will carry some of America's most significant founding-era documents. The tour is in honor of America's 250th anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris

item.title: Investigators still searching for answers after deadly Austin bar shooting
item.description: Authorities in Austin, Texas, are still searching for answers two days after a shooting at a bar left four dead, including the suspected shooter, and more than a dozen people injured.
item.content:encoded: Authorities in Austin, Texas, are still searching for answers two days after a shooting at a bar left four dead, including the suspected shooter, and more than a dozen people injured.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Weber

item.title: As more people turn to chatbots for health advice, studies say they may be led astray
item.description: People are turning to AI chatbots to help them with medical advice. Recent studies suggest these bots are not always helpful in making decisions about health.
item.content:encoded: People are turning to AI chatbots to help them with medical advice. Recent studies suggest these bots are not always helpful in making decisions about health.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle
